title: A Treaty Written in Thorns
tagline: Two enemy envoys are bound to a living treaty that punishes every lie they tell each other.

cast.name: Liora Ash
cast.role: Co-protagonist; disciplined Ash envoy whose strategic ambiguity becomes dangerous under the living treaty.
cast.visualDescription: Adult woman with an olive complexion and black hair braided into a formal crownless updo, wearing a structured deep-emerald envoy's gown with black-iron shoulder guards, parchment panels and restrained gold filigree; white blossoms and living thorn-ink circle one bare wrist.

cast.name: Ren Vale
cast.role: Co-protagonist; celebrated Vale commander learning to negotiate grief instead of defeating it.
cast.visualDescription: Broad-shouldered adult man with a tawny complexion, dark wavy hair and close beard, dressed in thorn-ridged blackened plate over a bruised-rose cloak and formal Vale sash; his peace-bound sword and vine-scarred wrist remain clearly visible.

storefront.tileSubtitle: Tell the truth across an enemy border
storefront.keyArtPrompt: Two rival fantasy envoys standing on opposite sides of a living vine-written treaty, disciplined woman diplomat in deep emerald ceremonial dress and celebrated man commander in thorned black armor, their wrists linked by flowering ink, burned frontier fields blooming behind them, bruised rose twilight, parchment cream, candlelit court intrigue, romantic tension without embrace, original characters, no text, vertical key art

prologueFrames.title: A Century of Ashes
paragraphs: For a hundred years, the kingdoms of Ash and Vale have traded the same border through war, truce, and war again. Their burned frontier now grows nothing except graves and thorn scrub.
Envoy Liora Ash and Commander Ren Vale arrive at the peace table carrying generations of grief, and courts that need them to end the fighting without changing who holds power.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: Two rival delegations face each other across a table overlooking a burned frontier.

prologueFrames.title: The Living Signature
paragraphs: The treaty is written in a ceremonial vine said to recognize honest intent. When Liora and Ren sign, green ink climbs beneath their skin and binds each diplomat to the other's words.
Their first courteous lie raises a thorn in both palms; their first painful truth opens a white flower in the wasteland outside.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: A living green treaty links Liora and Ren as one flower opens beyond the court windows.

prologueFrames.title: Peace Under Attack
paragraphs: Before either court can withdraw, soldiers bearing Vale colors attack an Ash border post and leave evidence too perfect to trust. Liora suspects Ren's command; Ren suspects her aunt Queen Maelin arranged a pretext.
Bound to feel every lie between them, the envoys begin a joint progress along the frontier to find who needs their treaty to fail.
prologueFrames.semanticLabel: Liora and Ren ride together toward a smoking border post as thorned ink circles their hands.

chapters.title: The Living Signature
chapters.caption: Sign a peace whose promises can answer back.
paragraphs: Before both courts, Liora and Ren arrange the treaty's living clauses so neither kingdom can hide an army inside a careful definition. They sign before either understands that the vine will punish private lies as fiercely as public ones.

The discarded loopholes twist together as The Covenant Briar, defending every ambiguity the courts hoped to exploit. Liora and Ren can cut through it only by stating what each side is truly afraid to lose.
chapters.semanticLabel: Liora and Ren sign a green living treaty at a candlelit diplomatic table.

encounters.name: Clausebloom Adder
encounters.visualDescription: A living-vine serpent with cream parchment scales, black-ink clauses along its spine and a white treaty flower opening inside a thorn-lined jaw.

encounters.name: Wax-Oath Scarab
encounters.visualDescription: A black-iron beetle plated in opposing emerald and bruised-rose seals, carrying an incomplete wax signature between calligraphy-nib mandibles.

chapters.title: Truth Under Skin
chapters.caption: Learn the cost of courtesy without honesty.
paragraphs: Their first private negotiation becomes a lesson in strategic honesty: omissions sting, polished evasions draw blood, and truths offered as weapons grow thorns too. Liora admits she expects Ren to betray her, while Ren admits he came prepared to deserve that fear.

Their rehearsed courtly answers take form as The Courtesy Shade, speaking each pleasing falsehood in a beloved voice. Naming the motives beneath those answers clears their skin and establishes the first rules they choose together.
chapters.semanticLabel: Thorn shadows rise behind Liora and Ren during a tense private negotiation.

encounters.name: Silken Alibi
encounters.visualDescription: A courtly shadow wrapped in bruised-rose silk, its smooth face rotating between flattering porcelain masks while thorned hands remain hidden behind its back.

encounters.name: Needleword Vine
encounters.visualDescription: A calligraphic green-black vine that coils into a speaking profile, with fine iron needles beneath translucent parchment skin and honeyed words blooming as poisonous flowers.

chapters.title: The Border Progress
chapters.caption: Investigate an attack designed to restart a war.
paragraphs: Liora and Ren tour the frontier under the guise of a ceremonial courtship, questioning soldiers who would never speak freely to an enemy envoy. At the ruined post, formation marks prove the attackers copied Ren's tactics but never served under his command.

A standard stitched from forged loyalties unfolds into The False-Flag Shrike and scatters evidence toward both kingdoms. The pair follow what it tries hardest to destroy, finding orders written in Spymaster Orr's private cipher.
chapters.semanticLabel: The envoys inspect a sabotaged border post while a false banner circles overhead.

encounters.name: Cipher-Banner Scissortail
encounters.visualDescription: A knife-winged black bird stitched from torn Ash and Vale standards, with a long forked banner tail and Orr's private cipher picked out in pale thread across its pinfeathers.

encounters.name: Marchline Decoy
encounters.visualDescription: A wooden-and-iron training soldier wearing mismatched halves of both armies' uniforms, stamping copied formation marks and backward footprints with jointed boot presses.

chapters.title: Flowers in Burned Fields
chapters.caption: Let shared grief restore contested ground.
paragraphs: Two villages claim the same surviving well, each with a memorial proving the other kingdom burned its fields. Liora and Ren stop arguing over whose history is complete and publicly name what both armies destroyed.

The unburied grief charges from the ash as The Cinderhart, carrying a different list of dead on every branch of its antlers. When both villages speak every name, flowers follow its path and reluctant trust takes root around a shared channel.
chapters.semanticLabel: White flowers spread across burned fields as two border villages gather by one well.

encounters.name: Memorial Fawn
encounters.visualDescription: A small deer of soot-black wood with two villages' memorial ribbons knotted around its legs and delicate white flowers beginning to open along its charred antlers.

encounters.name: Gravefurrow Boar
encounters.visualDescription: A low armored boar grown from burned roots, disputed boundary stones and broken plow iron, with one green shoot emerging wherever its tusks cut the ash.

chapters.title: The Masque of Two Courts
chapters.caption: Separate forged affection from a freely chosen truth.
paragraphs: At a diplomatic masque, forged messages implicate both royal families while every dance is treated as evidence of the envoys' staged romance. Liora and Ren set boundaries the treaty cannot choose for them, then admit that the attraction left outside those boundaries is real.

The masque's prescribed steps assemble into The Clockwork Chaperone, forcing every gesture toward a politically useful ending. Breaking its rhythm reveals the courier who planted Orr's letters and lets Liora and Ren leave the dance on their own terms.
chapters.semanticLabel: Masked courtiers surround Liora and Ren beneath flowering chandeliers and hidden forged letters.

encounters.name: Waltzmask Marionette
encounters.visualDescription: A gilded dance puppet in split emerald-and-rose court costume, with a two-faced masque, dance-card gears in its chest and black control wires pulling every limb into prescribed steps.

encounters.name: Letterlace Mayfly
encounters.visualDescription: A long-tailed black-lace mayfly whose four upright wings are forged love letters layered over parchment, with wax-seal eyes and flowering ink dust falling from each beat.

chapters.title: Thorns at the Heart
chapters.caption: Face the truths both crowns buried before peace.
paragraphs: The vine tightens toward Liora's and Ren's hearts as their courts issue incompatible orders. Liora learns Queen Maelin concealed early Ash provocations, while Ren refuses a Vale command to mobilize troops behind the progress.

Every old justification ignites inside The War-Ember Regent, a crowned furnace that calls inherited violence duty. Liora confronts her aunt and Ren releases his command, starving the regent of the obedience it expects.
chapters.semanticLabel: Liora and Ren face burning royal orders while thorns climb toward their hearts.

encounters.name: Edict Pyre
encounters.visualDescription: A walking crown-shaped brazier in black iron, its body draped in burning royal orders and its orange core fed by miniature emerald and rose war banners.

encounters.name: Heartbind Standard
encounters.visualDescription: A living military standard rooted on thorn legs, bearing two faded crowns while red-green vines reach from its embroidered heart toward the nearest oath scars.

chapters.title: Orr's Last Proof
chapters.caption: Expose a conspiracy built to look inevitable.
paragraphs: Orr arranges an assassination attempt whose evidence points cleanly to both kingdoms, certain that frightened courts will accept contradiction as proof of treachery. Liora and Ren retrace the conspiracy together, matching forged dispatches to the people ordered to carry them.

Beneath the council chamber, The Proof-Eater consumes any fact that joins one side's story to the other. The envoys feed it Orr's isolated lies in the right sequence, forcing the missing connections back into view before both courts.
chapters.semanticLabel: Liora and Ren present linked evidence before two alarmed royal courts.

encounters.name: Contradiction Forkmouth
encounters.visualDescription: A broad crawling ink blot split into two opposed crested mouths, with hooked writing limbs and a lightless hollow belly where linked testimony disappears.

encounters.name: Cipher Shearer
encounters.visualDescription: A long-legged black-iron evidence cutter with Orr's cipher engraved on scissor forelimbs, built to snip glowing witness threads without disturbing the isolated papers.

chapters.title: A Treaty Without Chains
chapters.caption: Replace two bound envoys with a peace everyone can own.
paragraphs: With Orr exposed, both crowns demand that Liora and Ren preserve the magical bond as the price of peace. Instead, the envoys gather border towns, veterans, traders, and grieving families to negotiate terms no pair of exceptional people must carry alone.

The old treaty closes around them as The Sovereign Knot, a living constellation of crowns and compulsory vows. They dissolve it clause by clause, then freely choose both a public peace and a private future that no magic can enforce.
chapters.semanticLabel: Delegates from both kingdoms surround Liora and Ren as the thorn bond blooms and falls away.

encounters.name: Crownchain Gyre
encounters.visualDescription: Two empty miniature crowns orbit a white-hot compulsory vow, linked by gold thorn chains against a deep emerald-and-rose star field shaped like a closed fist.

encounters.name: Bound-Vote Herald
encounters.visualDescription: A faceless dual-court herald in layered parchment robes, carrying two chained blank signatures while many translucent delegate hands strain outward from beneath its sleeves.

finaleFrames.title: The Frontier Council
paragraphs: The new treaty is copied into ordinary ink and entrusted to a council drawn from the people who live with its consequences. Its clauses can be challenged, amended, and renewed without drawing anyone's blood.

Queen Maelin relinquishes the powers she hid behind necessity, and Ren's former soldiers help clear the roads they once fortified. Across the border, spring reaches fields that had forgotten its color.
finaleFrames.semanticLabel: A diverse frontier council signs an ordinary parchment as green fields return outside.

finaleFrames.title: Chosen Without Magic
paragraphs: Weeks later, Liora meets Ren at the well where the first flowers grew. Neither bears a thorn, and neither kingdom has ordered them to be there.

They tell each other what they want without pain forcing the answer, then walk the reopened border road side by side. The flowers that follow are only flowers, which makes them beautiful in a new way.
finaleFrames.semanticLabel: Liora and Ren meet freely beside a flower-ringed frontier well at sunrise.
